2016-03-26.abstract: Amid rising concerns about extremism, a 28-year-old petition supporting secularism has been revived, and the country’s Supreme Court is to hear the challenge on Sunday.

2016-03-26.title: Belgium Fears Nuclear Plants Are Vulnerable
2016-03-26.abstract: Belgian authorities fear that Islamic State militants may try to attack, infiltrate or sabotage nuclear plants or obtain nuclear or radioactive material.

2016-03-26.title: Headed to U.S. for a Belated Celebration, Jennifer Scintu-Waetzmann Dies in Brussels Attacks
2016-03-26.abstract: Jennifer Scintu-Waetzmann and her husband were traveling to New York City for their first anniversary. She died in the attack, and he is in a coma.

2016-03-26.title: North Korean Propaganda Video Depicts Nuclear Strike on Washington
2016-03-26.abstract: The video uploaded Saturday includes a warning to “American imperialists” not to provoke the North.

2016-03-26.title: 1916: Rebels Rise Up in Dublin
2016-03-26.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: Irish nationalists seized locations in the city during Easter in 1916.

2016-03-27.title: After an Indictment, Turks Give U.S. Prosecutor a Hero’s Welcome Online
2016-03-27.abstract: A Twitter post announcing charges against the tycoon Reza Zarrab has made Preet Bharara, U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, a social media sensation.

2016-03-27.title: 1916: Pancho Villa Is Surrounded by Americans
2016-03-27.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: The Mexican chieftain and 200 of his followers sought refuge in the mountains as American troops neared in 1916.

2016-03-27.title: Syrian Troops Said to Recapture Historic Palmyra From ISIS
2016-03-27.abstract: The end of the ancient city’s nearly yearlong occupation brought a rare measure of international praise for President Bashar al-Assad.

2016-03-27.title: 1966: World Cup Trophy Found By Briton Walking His Dog
2016-03-27.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: The trophy that England won in the 1966 World Cup, which was stolen from an exhibition, was discovered near London.

2016-03-27.title: Tensions Erupt in Brussels, and Police in 4 Countries Make Arrests
2016-03-27.abstract: Memorials for the victims of the terrorist attacks in Brussels were interrupted on Sunday by far-right protesters.

2016-03-28.title: Bangladesh Editor Faces 79 Court Cases After an Unusual Confession
2016-03-28.abstract: After admitting it was wrong to have years ago published allegations against the prime minister, Mahfuz Anam could be sentenced to 175 years in prison.

2016-03-28.title: Who Will Become a Terrorist? Research Yields Few Clues
2016-03-28.abstract: The question of what turns people toward violence — and whether they can be steered away from it — has bedeviled governments around the world for generations.

2016-03-28.title: An Ancient Caravan Town in China Is Reborn
2016-03-28.abstract: In a project praised by Unesco, a Swiss-led team spent years renovating the square of Sideng Village, with an eye toward historical consistency that is rare in China.

2016-03-28.title: French Journalist Is Detained at U.N. War Crimes Tribunal
2016-03-28.abstract: Florence Hartmann, a former correspondent for Le Monde, has been locked up at the tribunal prison to serve a pending seven-day sentence for contempt of court.

2016-03-28.title: Man Arrested as ‘Third Bomber’ in Brussels Attack Is Freed; Hunt Is Renewed
2016-03-28.abstract: The police, appealing to the public for help, said the real assailant remained at large.

2016-03-29.title: Central African Republic: More Peacekeepers Accused
2016-03-29.abstract: The United Nations is investigating two new suspected cases of sexual abuse committed by peacekeepers in the Central African Republic.

2016-03-29.title: Argentina: President Won’t Fund Leftist TV Network
2016-03-29.abstract: Argentina’s president, Mauricio Macri, will cut its funding of a regional television network started by the Venezuelan government in 2005.

2016-03-29.title: Scenes From Palmyra Indicate ISIS Slowed Assault on Treasures
2016-03-29.abstract: A day after Syrian troops recaptured the city, television footage showed severe damage to ancient sculptures. But there is hope that many works can be reconstructed.

2016-03-29.title: If All Else Fails, 3D Models and Robots Might Rebuild Palmyra
2016-03-29.abstract: Cultural organizations have been working to create precise 3D digital models of the threatened heritage monuments in Palmyra, Syria, in case the originals are damaged beyond repair.

2016-03-29.title: Editor Says He Is Resigning Over Media Controls in China
2016-03-29.abstract: An editor at Southern Metropolis Daily posted a message online saying he could no longer “follow your surname,” a reference to the Communist Party.

2016-03-30.title: ISIS Fighters Laid Mines Around Palmyra’s Ancient Ruins Before Retreating, Syrians Say
2016-03-30.abstract: Islamic State fighters were reported to have booby-trapped Palmyra’s archaeological treasures before retreating.

2016-03-30.title: Emergency Over Ebola Has Ended, W.H.O. Says
2016-03-30.abstract: West African countries can contain the few new cases that arise, and “the likelihood of international spread is low,” the World Health Organization concluded.

2016-03-30.title: As Nuclear Security Summit Begins, Materials Remain Vulnerable to Theft
2016-03-30.abstract: As President Obama gathers leaders for his last Nuclear Security Summit, tons of materials that terrorists could use to make small nuclear devices or dirty bombs remain vulnerable to theft.

2016-03-30.title: Tribunal Releases Journalist Held 5 Days in a Prison for War Criminals
2016-03-30.abstract: Florence Hartmann, from France, was held on a 2009 warrant for contempt of court, for writing about the confidential workings of tribunal judges.

2016-03-30.title: Johanna Atlegrim, Swedish Illustrator, Dies in Brussels Attacks
2016-03-30.abstract: Ms. Atlegrim, 30, who lived in Brussels and recently illustrated the cover of a children’s magazine, died in the attack on the Maelbeek subway station.

2016-03-31.title: Key Quotes, in Wiradjuri
2016-03-31.abstract: “The world does not respect people who have no language.” (Pronounced: Moon-im-bin-ah-lah nur-em-bung-gah wi-ray yin-duh-mah-lah wi-ray my-nee-yung-de-ray)

2016-03-31.title: Key Quotes, in Wiradjuri
2016-03-31.abstract: “I have done my work. I am finished.” PHONETIC SPELLING TK

2016-03-31.title: Key Quotes, in Wiradjuri
2016-03-31.abstract: “I have done my work. I am finished.” (Pronounced: Nah-du bee-yung-go-nah gah-don-bee-yay bal-do-gor-ee-gi-dahn)

2016-03-31.title: Key Quotes, in Wiradjuri
2016-03-31.abstract: “My grandfather was a law man.” (Pronounced: Moo-mah-lah-di boo-ya do-ray mine)

2016-03-31.title: Paul Pontallier, Who Gave New Life to a Bordeaux Estate, Dies at 59
2016-03-31.abstract: Mr. Pontallier helped restore Château Margaux, a celebrated name in the history of wine, to pre-eminence and served as its passionate embodiment.
